description: Recommended YouTube channels and podcasts for Level 0 learners. Curated for: slow speech, clear pronunciation, beginner vocabulary, visual support, and structured lessons.

primary_channels.name: BBC Learning English
primary_channels.why: World-class beginner content. Clear British + American pronunciation. 6 Minute English series is perfect for L0-L1 transition.

primary_channels.name: Rachel's English
primary_channels.why: THE best channel for American pronunciation. Mouth close-ups show tongue/lip position. Slow, clear explanations.

primary_channels.name: English with Lucy
primary_channels.why: Excellent vocabulary and grammar for beginners. Clear speech. Well-structured lessons.

primary_channels.name: EngVid
primary_channels.why: Multiple teachers, whiteboard style. Good for grammar patterns. Simple explanations.
primary_channels.daily_use: Grammar reference for weekly pattern cards

supplementary_channels.name: English Singsing
supplementary_channels.why: Songs and dialogues for absolute beginners. Repetitive. Good for Week 1-3.

supplementary_channels.name: Easy English
supplementary_channels.why: Real conversations with subtitles. Street interviews with slow speakers.

supplementary_channels.name: TED-Ed
supplementary_channels.why: Animated educational videos. Clear narration. Visual support. Good for L0-L1 transition.

primary_podcasts.name: 6 Minute English
primary_podcasts.why: 6-minute episodes. Clear speech. Vocabulary explained. Transcript available.
primary_podcasts.use: 1 episode per week. Listen 2-3 times. Identify new words. Shadow key sentences.

primary_podcasts.name: ESL Pod (English as a Second Language Podcast)
primary_podcasts.why: Specifically designed for beginners. VERY slow. Each episode teaches specific vocabulary with context.
primary_podcasts.use: Daily listening if available. Perfect speed for L0 shadowing.

primary_podcasts.name: Voice of America Learning English
primary_podcasts.why: News and stories at slow speed. Clear American pronunciation. Real content at beginner pace.
primary_podcasts.use: 1-2 episodes per week for extended listening practice.

how_to_use_podcasts.step_1: Listen once without stopping (get the general idea)
how_to_use_podcasts.step_2: Listen again — pause after each sentence and repeat it
how_to_use_podcasts.step_3: Read transcript if available — find 5 new words
how_to_use_podcasts.step_5: Summarize what you heard in 2-3 sentences (spoken, recorded)

real_life_exposure_tasks.description: Progressive real-world English tasks that move learners from controlled practice to authentic usage.

weekly_challenges.task: Change your phone language to English for 1 hour. What words do you recognize?
Read 3 English product labels in your kitchen. Write what each product is.
Listen to 1 English song on the radio/Spotify. What words can you catch?
Watch 5 minutes of an English YouTube video. What is the topic? Write 3 words you heard.
Order something in English (at a coffee shop, online chat, or practice alone). Record yourself.
Ask for directions in English (to a friend who speaks English, or practice with yourself/AI). Record.
Read an English social media post (Instagram, Twitter). Write a 1-sentence response.
Have a 2-minute conversation in English with someone (friend, family member, or AI chatbot). Record it.

real_life_exposure_tasks.principle: Each week pushes slightly beyond the classroom into real English use. Even 'failures' are learning — the goal is TRYING, not perfection.

posting_to_discord.format: Bot posts this week's recommended clip + podcast every Monday
posting_to_discord.format_2: Pinned message with all recommended podcasts + how to use them
